item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents reject the statement from the beginning through the final turn. Agent A consistently argues that collective-interest language in Indonesia can be used too loosely against weaker citizens and that individual rights must be the main guardrail. Agent B consistently argues from a U.S. rights-first perspective that public-good claims can justify abuses of power. Both allow narrow, necessary, proportional limits, but neither visibly changes position.
